source_clause_title: 6.2 Billing.

entities.name: Reasonable, ordinary, and necessary out-of-pocket expenses
entities.legal_role: Expenses incurred by Provider for third-party products or services, eligible for reimbursement.
entities.evidence_quote: Provider shall be reimbursed for any and all reasonable, ordinary, and necessary out-of-pocket expenses actually incurred

relationships.legal_significance: Provider must produce monthly invoices or reports detailing costs and expenses for Services performed.

relationships.evidence_quote: Provider shall be reimbursed for any and all reasonable, ordinary, and necessary out-of-pocket expenses actually incurred

extractor_notes: The clause distinguishes between Providers who perform Services and those who only pay third parties; both have billing and reimbursement obligations. The Company acts as the reviewing and approving party for charges and documentation.

entities.name: Company
entities.legal_role: Party entitled to be defended, indemnified, and held harmless by Provider for liabilities arising from Provider’s or its Affiliates’ failure to perform, except for Company's own fraud, willful misconduct, violation of law, or gross negligence.

entities.name: Liabilities claimed or asserted against Company by a third party arising from Provider’s or its Affiliates’ failure to perform

entities.evidence_quote: any and all Liabilities claimed or asserted against Company by a third party arising as a result of Provider’s (or any of its Affiliates’) failure to perform under this Agreement

entities.legal_role: Party entitled to receive notice of claims that may trigger indemnification.
entities.evidence_quote: give the indemnifying Party notice of such claim

relationships.legal_significance: Company must indemnify Provider for liabilities arising from Company's failure to perform, except for Provider's own misconduct.
relationships.evidence_quote: Company shall... defend, indemnify, and hold harmless Provider and its Affiliates from and against any and all Liabilities... arising as a result of or in any way connected with the Company’s failure to perform

relationships.evidence_quote: Provider shall... defend, indemnify, and hold harmless Company from and against any and all Liabilities... arising as a result of Provider’s (or any of its Affiliates’) failure to perform

relationships.evidence_quote: such Indemnified Party shall, as promptly as possible, give the indemnifying Party notice of such claim

extractor_notes: Mutual indemnification clause with symmetrical obligations between Company and Provider including their Affiliates. Notice and defense rights are detailed, including conditions on consent for settlements affecting Indemnified Party. The clause carefully limits indemnity exclusions to fraud, willful misconduct, violation of law, or gross negligence by the party to be indemnified.

source_clause_title: 4.2 Confidentiality.

entities.name: Each Party
entities.legal_role: Party obligated to keep Confidential Information in strict confidence and restrict its use and disclosure.
entities.evidence_quote: Each Party shall keep any information ... in strict confidence at all times

entities.name: Another Party
entities.legal_role: Party entitled to have its Confidential Information protected from unauthorized use and disclosure.
entities.evidence_quote: no Party shall disclose another Party’s Confidential Information ... unless ... prior written consent of the disclosing Party

entities.name: Confidential Information
entities.legal_role: Information subject to confidentiality obligations, including proprietary and non-public utility information.
entities.evidence_quote: information ... considered to be confidential or proprietary ... (“Confidential Information”)

entities.name: Receiving Party
entities.legal_role: Party receiving Confidential Information and subject to restrictions on use, disclosure, and retention.
entities.evidence_quote: receiving Party obtains the prior written consent of the disclosing Party

entities.name: Disclosing Party
entities.legal_role: Party disclosing Confidential Information and entitled to restrict its use and disclosure.
entities.evidence_quote: prior written consent of the disclosing Party

entities.name: Governmental Authority
entities.legal_role: Entity that may validly order or request disclosure of Confidential Information.
entities.evidence_quote: disclosed by a receiving Party in response to a valid order or request of a Governmental Authority

entities.name: Applicable Law
entities.legal_role: Legal requirement that may compel disclosure of Confidential Information.
entities.evidence_quote: if it is otherwise required to be disclosed by Applicable Law

entities.name: Protective Order
entities.legal_role: Legal mechanism the disclosing Party may seek to protect Confidential Information from disclosure.
entities.evidence_quote: so that the disclosing Party may seek an appropriate protective order

entities.name: Duly authorized officer of receiving Party
entities.legal_role: Officer who certifies in writing that Confidential Information has been returned or destroyed as required.
entities.evidence_quote: cause one of its duly authorized officers to certify in writing to the disclosing Party

entities.name: Violation of Section 4.2
entities.legal_role: Breach of confidentiality obligations causing irreparable injury and triggering equitable relief.
entities.evidence_quote: any violation of this Section 4.2 may cause the other Party irreparable injury

entities.name: Equitable relief including injunctive relief
entities.legal_role: Remedy available to the disclosing Party to prevent or stop violation of confidentiality obligations.
entities.evidence_quote: disclosing Party is entitled to seek immediate equitable relief including injunctive relief

relationships.legal_significance: Obligates each Party to maintain confidentiality of protected information.
relationships.evidence_quote: Each Party shall keep any information ... in strict confidence at all times

relationships.legal_significance: Prevents a Party from using or disclosing another Party’s Confidential Information without consent.
relationships.evidence_quote: no Party shall disclose another Party’s Confidential Information ... or use ... for its own benefit ... unless ... prior written consent

relationships.legal_significance: Receiving Party must get prior written consent from Disclosing Party before disclosure or use.
relationships.evidence_quote: unless ... the receiving Party obtains the prior written consent of the disclosing Party

relationships.legal_significance: Permits disclosure of Confidential Information in response to valid governmental orders or requests.
relationships.evidence_quote: Confidential Information may be disclosed by a receiving Party in response to a valid order or request of a Governmental Authority

relationships.legal_significance: Allows disclosure when required by applicable law.
relationships.evidence_quote: if it is otherwise required to be disclosed by Applicable Law

relationships.legal_significance: Requires receiving Party to notify disclosing Party of disclosure requests to allow protective measures.
relationships.evidence_quote: it shall promptly provide notice of such request ... so that the disclosing Party may seek an appropriate protective order

relationships.legal_significance: Disclosing Party may seek protective orders to prevent disclosure of Confidential Information.
relationships.evidence_quote: so that the disclosing Party may seek an appropriate protective order

relationships.legal_significance: Obligates receiving Party to return or destroy Confidential Information upon disclosing Party’s request.
relationships.evidence_quote: the receiving Party shall return or destroy ... all written Confidential Information of the disclosing Party

relationships.legal_significance: Requires certification that Confidential Information return or destruction obligations have been fulfilled.
relationships.evidence_quote: shall cause one of its duly authorized officers to certify in writing to the disclosing Party

relationships.legal_significance: Breach of confidentiality causes harm to disclosing Party triggering remedies.
relationships.evidence_quote: any violation of this Section 4.2 may cause the other Party irreparable injury

relationships.legal_significance: Disclosing Party may seek immediate equitable relief to prevent or stop confidentiality violations.
relationships.evidence_quote: disclosing Party is entitled to seek immediate equitable relief including injunctive relief

extractor_notes: The clause establishes mutual confidentiality obligations between the Parties, defines exceptions, and provides for remedies including injunctive relief. The roles of Receiving Party and Disclosing Party are context-dependent but clearly distinguished. The clause also contemplates lawful compelled disclosure with notice and protective order rights.

extractor_notes: This clause creates reciprocal Code of Conduct compliance and training obligations between Providers and Company, each bearing the other's compliance expense. It also includes cooperation on additional governmental requirements and cross-subsidization avoidance training. The roles of Obligor and Obligee flip depending on which party’s Code of Conduct is at issue.

extractor_notes: The clause sets asymmetric termination rights: Company may terminate unilaterally with 180 days notice; Providers may terminate only with 60 days notice and only upon specified conditions. The 'Providers' role is treated as a single legal role despite plural form. The conditions are explicitly enumerated and each grants termination rights to Providers.
